header.title: Daily FI Market Intelligence

header.stance: 美國固定收益市場休市；全球政策分歧主導下一個開市交易日

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 假日固定收益簡報

美國固定收益市場於 7 月 3 日星期五因美國獨立紀念日（補假）休市，符合 SIFMA 的全日休市建議。當日沒有美國固定收益市場的正常交易時段，也沒有形成當日的美國固定收益價格。相關資訊窗口從 7 月 3 日亞洲早盤延續至 7 月 6 日亞洲早盤，位於星期四提前收市之後、星期一恢復正常交易之前。這段期間的發展可能影響市場重開，但不能將其歸因於 7 月 3 日的美國公債走勢。

這個窗口的主要發展是：隨著伊朗相關能源衝擊消退，全球貨幣政策風險的分歧擴大。油價下跌及美國招聘疲弱，降低了近期進一步緊縮的急迫性，多位 ECB 官員也認為 6 月的升息目前已經足夠。然而，這場衝擊仍提高了主要經濟體的通膨門檻，而財政擴張、人工智慧投資及央行指引減少，則使較長存續期間的補償持續承壓。因此，美國市場於星期一重開時，必須消化近期能源壓力減輕與仍具限制性且異常分散的政策路徑之間的矛盾。

主要的反向力量來自日本。當地的正產出缺口、日圓疲弱及政府債券壓力，使政策正常化風險持續存在，即使油價下跌在邊際上減輕了輸入性通膨。中國也減少主權債購買，同時增設更精準的隔夜流動性工具，顯示其支持資金融通，卻不認可無上限的存續期間需求。美國總統 Donald Trump 的盟友再次試圖重塑 Federal Reserve，則增添了另一層制度不確定性。這些發展共同使市場重開時，同時對成長與通膨數據，以及政策反應函數的可信度保持敏感。

## 全球利率、信用與主權債發展

歐洲提供了最明確的證據，顯示能源通膨消退正在改變近期政策辯論。法國、德國及義大利通膨普遍減速後，歐元區通膨放緩至 2.8%，低於預期。ECB Governing Council 成員 Emmanuel Moulin 表示，油價快速下跌，使央行在 6 月一致決議升息 25 個基點至 2.25% 後處於更有利的位置；他並強調，這次行動並未被定位為新一輪緊縮週期的起點。Joachim Nagel 仍敦促保持警覺，Gabriel Makhlouf 則強調對 2% 目標的承諾。這項分歧使通膨延後轉嫁至食品、服務及薪資的程度，成為延長暫停升息最關鍵的限制。

成長與外部平衡數據削弱了這項有利存續期間的動能。法國 5 月製造業產出下降 1.0%，此前增幅修正為 0.6%；運輸設備，以及電機、電子與電腦產品疲弱，是主要拖累。整體工業產出下滑 0.1%，但採礦、能源及供水上升 3.2%，使訊號並非全面疲弱。EU 第一季經常帳盈餘擴大至 €113.4 billion，相當於 GDP 的 2.4%，因服務及初次所得增強的影響，超過商品盈餘縮小。法國生產轉弱支持政策保持耐心，但外部盈餘限制了歐元區出現廣泛融資壓力的論點。

法國財政展望帶來另一種主權債存續期間風險。Finance Minister Roland Lescure 正尋求將赤字降至接近 GDP 的 5%，相較 2025 年的 5.1% 僅小幅改善。Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu 警告，若未通過新財政法案、而是仰賴沿用既有法規，赤字可能升至 6.5%。2027 年預算必須通過政黨版圖分裂的國會，並面對預定於 4 月舉行的總統大選，使財政整頓容易延誤或陷入混亂。官員表示，發債與標售運作良好，因此眼前問題並非市場准入，而是如果政治妥協無法形成可信的債務路徑，未來市場將要求多高的溢價。

Federal Reserve 的不確定性由下一次決策轉向治理與溝通。6 月非農就業僅增加 57,000 人，且先前數據向下修正，已削弱立即升息的理由，儘管勞動參與率下降使失業率下滑更難解讀。假日期間，有報導指出，在 Supreme Court 阻止針對 Lisa Cook 的行動後，政府盟友正探索其他罷免理事的方式，使央行獨立性問題再度浮現。Chair Kevin Warsh 減少依賴前瞻指引又增添一層變數：政策訊號減少可能提高新公布數據的影響力，而政治壓力則可能引發外界疑慮，擔心央行能否堅定因應通膨風險。

日本呈現溫和的超額需求，政策組合也承受明顯壓力。BOJ 估計第一季產出缺口為 0.53%，略低於 0.57%，資本與勞動均有正向貢獻。其潛在成長率估計由 0.66% 升至 0.69%，但央行提醒，兩項衡量指標都容易受到方法論影響。Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama 重申，已準備因應失序的匯率波動，並將日本殖利率上升與美國政策訊號、美國勞動數據疲弱及國內標售表現不佳連結起來。10 年期 JGB 殖利率升至 2.81%，創 1996 年以來最高，顯示油價下跌並未取代市場對財政、匯率及買方基礎的疑慮。

中國的流動性立場顯示，其重點是控制，而非透過債券市場廣泛寬鬆。PBOC 於 6 月淨買入 10 billion yuan 政府債券，低於 5 月的 50 billion yuan，為九個月來最少。其新的隔夜公開市場操作，為政策制定者提供期限更短的資金融通調節工具，降低透過主權債購買注入長期流動性的必要性。這項組合既能維持充裕的現金環境，也能避免殖利率過度壓縮；同時將支持放緩中的國內經濟，與承諾不計代價承接存續期間區分開來。

初級信用市場融資管道仍然暢通，較重要的訊號來自潛在供給的結構與時點。CVC 計畫在夏季過後發行 €1.2 billion 高收益債券，取代其收購義大利食品配料製造商 Irca 所使用的銀行過橋融資，並搭配一項循環融資額度。這筆交易將為仍由再融資及重新定價主導的歐洲市場，增加槓桿收購供給，即使投資級以下債券需求依然穩健。ICICI Bank 另正考慮未來幾週內，在其全球中期票據計畫下發行至少 $500 million，運用 RBI 為吸引境外資本而設計的外匯交換優惠。兩項規劃中的交易目前都尚未確立成交條件；但都顯示借款人正準備利用融資窗口開放的機會。

新興市場的通膨及融資環境仍然高度因國而異。土耳其 6 月 CPI 月增 0.99%、年增 32.11%，但食品通膨達 35.45%，住房與公用事業通膨達 45.14%，說明為何僅憑能源價格下跌，可能不足以穩定通膨下降路徑。與此同時，隨著美國政策及美元的不確定性上升，投資人已將利差交易的融資來源由美元分散至歐元、加幣及日圓。這降低了對單一融資貨幣的依賴，但 Fed 若再度展現緊縮信號，仍可能縮小利率差並收緊高收益主權債的融資環境。巴西則透過對非正式工作者提供新的信貸及債務協商支持，引入財政與貨幣政策疑問，即使官員主張這些措施不會削弱貨幣政策的限制性。

## 下一個開市交易日觀察

美國第一波總體經濟數據將於 7 月 6 日星期一美東時間上午 10:00 公布，包括 ISM 服務業指數及 New York Fed 的 Global Supply Chain Pressure Index。5 月貿易數據將於星期二公布，同日還有 6 月 Survey of Consumer Expectations；6 月 16–17 日 FOMC 會議紀要則於星期三下午 2:00 公布。服務業活動轉弱、價格降溫及物流壓力受控，將使非農就業成長疲弱的訊息延伸至 2 年期至 5 年期的政策傳導區段。若需求展現韌性或價格壓力再起，尤其是會議紀要顯示廣泛支持在 2026 年升息，將恢復更具限制性的解讀，並削弱能源壓力緩解足以支撐市場重開的論點。

Treasury 公布的供給日程，將殖利率曲線前端的現金需求與指標券存續期間的承接情況區分開來。星期一將標售 $92 billion 的 91 天期國庫券及 $79 billion 的 182 天期國庫券；星期二另有 $90 billion 的 42 天期國庫券、$52 billion 的 364 天期國庫券，以及 $58 billion 的 3 年期公債。星期三接著增額發行 $39 billion、票息 4.375% 的 10 年期公債，星期四再增額發行 $22 billion、票息 5.000% 的 30 年期公債。投標倍數、投標人配售及標售後表現，將顯示假日交割週期的影響是僅限於現金管理，還是延伸至長端期限溢價。參與廣泛將緩和供給疑慮；交易商大量承接或債券持續走弱則會加深疑慮。

日本接下來公布的數據，將決定正產出缺口是否正轉化為持久的所得與通膨壓力。5 月初步勞動數據將於 7 月 7 日公布，隨後是 6 月 BOJ 操作、政府交易及 5 月消費指數；BOJ 地區經濟報告則預定於 7 月 9 日發布。現金所得穩健、實質薪資改善及各地區價格壓力擴大，將提高政策正常化即使在油價下跌之下仍須推進的可能性，其中 JGB 存續期間及日圓最為敏感。若家庭所得或消費疲弱，加上證據顯示流動性變化很大一部分源自資產負債表運作機制，將挑戰偏鷹解讀，並降低日本對全球期限溢價壓力的影響。
zh-TW.methodology_note: SIFMA 建議美國固定收益市場於美國獨立紀念日（補假）全日休市。本文不將前一交易日的市場水準呈現為當日變動。

overall_summary.headline: 美國市場重開前，能源壓力緩解與持續的政策分歧交織
overall_summary.session_narrative: 美國固定收益市場因美國獨立紀念日（補假）休市，當日未形成美國市場價格。從亞洲至下一個亞洲交易時段的資訊窗口來看，能源壓力消退及美國招聘疲弱，降低了近期緊縮的急迫性；但日本的正產出缺口、日圓疲弱及債券壓力，仍使政策正常化風險延續。中國選擇精準的融資支持，而非廣泛提振存續期間需求；Fed 治理疑慮再起，也提高了政策可信度所需的溢價。下一個開市交易日必須消化近期通膨壓力減輕與政策路徑分散且仍具限制性之間的矛盾。
overall_summary.summary: 油價下跌、美國招聘轉弱及歐元區通膨降溫，緩解了近期緊縮壓力。日本的政策正常化風險、中國受控的流動性支持，以及 Fed 治理的不確定性，使全球存續期間補償持續承壓。

section_summaries.market_read: 市場重開時，將面對較溫和的即期通膨壓力，但政策風險溢價更為堅挺。
section_summaries.next_check: 週一的服務業數據將檢驗招聘疲弱是否延伸至殖利率曲線前端的政策路徑。
section_summaries.summary: 美國市場休市期間，這個資訊窗口沒有帶來國內市場的價格發現。能源壓力消退與招聘轉弱，降低了近期緊縮的急迫性；但日本的政策正常化風險、中國選擇性的流動性支持，以及 Fed 獨立性疑慮，仍使較長存續期間的補償容易承壓。

section_summaries.market_read: 全球寬鬆預期仍受主權、匯率及可信度風險制約。
section_summaries.next_check: 歐洲薪資轉嫁效應及法國預算路徑，將決定政策耐心能否延續。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球利率、信用與主權債發展
section_summaries.summary: 歐元區通膨降溫與法國產出轉弱，有利於 ECB 保持耐心；但法國財政脆弱性及日本債券持續承壓，限制了有利存續期間的訊號。中國控制較短期流動性，加上初級信用市場融資管道暢通，顯示資金融通獲得支持，卻未對主權債存續期間形成全面買盤。

section_summaries.market_read: 數據與標售承接情況將決定市場重開壓力是停留於前端，還是向外擴散。
section_summaries.next_check: 留意 10 年期與 30 年期標售的投標倍數、投標人配售及標售後交易表現。
section_summaries.section_title: 下一個開市交易日觀察
section_summaries.summary: 假期過後，下一個美國交易日必須消化密集的總體經濟與標售日程。服務業活動、供應鏈壓力及 FOMC 會議紀要將檢驗近期政策路徑；長端標售需求及日本所得數據，則將揭示期限溢價壓力是否擴大。

watch_events.market_implication: 偏鷹的組合將強化 2 年期至 5 年期殖利率的限制性；數據疲弱則會緩解限制。
watch_events.market_trigger: 需求展現韌性、價格壓力再起，或廣泛支持在 2026 年升息。
watch_events.title: 美國服務業、通膨及 FOMC 會議紀要
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤服務業需求、價格壓力、物流狀況及 FOMC 在 6 月的政策辯論。

watch_events.market_implication: 需求疲弱將推升長端期限溢價壓力；參與廣泛則會緩和壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 交易商大量承接，或 10 年期與 30 年期增額發行持續走弱。
watch_events.title: 美國公債指標券供給承接
watch_events.watch_line: 評估各場標售的投標倍數、投標人配售及標售後表現。

watch_events.market_implication: 這項組合將提高 BOJ 政策正常化風險，壓抑 JGB 存續期間，並使日圓維持高度敏感。
watch_events.market_trigger: 現金所得穩健、實質薪資改善，且日本各地區價格壓力擴大。
watch_events.title: 日本所得與政策正常化考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 關注現金所得、實質薪資、消費及 BOJ 對各地區價格的評估。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Holiday Fixed-Income Brief

U.S. fixed-income markets were closed on Friday, July 3, for U.S. Independence Day (observed), consistent with SIFMA’s full-close recommendation. There was no regular U.S. fixed-income session and no same-day U.S. fixed-income price discovery. The relevant information window runs from Asia morning on July 3 through Asia morning on July 6, after Thursday’s early close and before regular trading resumes Monday. Developments in that interval can shape the reopening, but they cannot be assigned to a July 3 Treasury move.

The window’s primary development was a widening divergence in global monetary-policy risks as the Iran-related energy shock faded. Lower oil and soft U.S. hiring reduced the urgency of additional near-term tightening, and several ECB voices judged June’s increase sufficient for now. The shock has nevertheless left a higher inflation hurdle across major economies, while fiscal expansion, artificial-intelligence investment and reduced central-bank guidance keep longer-duration compensation exposed. Monday’s U.S. reopening must therefore reconcile easier near-term energy pressure with policy paths that remain restrictive and unusually dispersed.

The main counterforce came from Japan, where a positive output gap, a weak yen and pressure in government bonds kept normalization risk alive even as lower oil reduced imported inflation at the margin. China also reduced sovereign-bond purchases while adding a more precise overnight liquidity tool, signaling support for funding without endorsing unlimited duration demand. Renewed attempts by President Donald Trump’s allies to reshape the Federal Reserve added a separate institutional uncertainty. Together, those developments leave the reopening sensitive to both the growth-and-inflation data and the credibility of the policy reaction function.

## Global Rates, Credit, And Sovereign Developments

Europe supplied the clearest evidence that energy disinflation is changing the near-term policy debate. Euro-area inflation slowed to 2.8%, below expectations, after broad deceleration in France, Germany and Italy. ECB Governing Council member Emmanuel Moulin said the rapid oil decline placed the central bank in a better position after June’s unanimous 25-basis-point increase to 2.25%, and emphasized that the move was not presented as the start of a new tightening cycle. Joachim Nagel still urged vigilance, while Gabriel Makhlouf stressed the commitment to the 2% target. The split turns delayed pass-through into food, services and wages into the decisive limitation on an extended pause.

Growth and external-balance data qualified that duration-friendly impulse. French manufacturing output fell 1.0% in May after a revised 0.6% increase, led by weakness in transport equipment and electrical, electronic and computer goods. Total industrial output slipped 0.1%, although mining, energy and water supply rose 3.2%, preventing a uniformly weak signal. The EU’s first-quarter current-account surplus widened to €113.4 billion, or 2.4% of GDP, as stronger services and primary income outweighed a smaller goods surplus. Softer French production supports patience, whereas the external surplus limits the case for broad euro-area funding stress.

France’s fiscal outlook created a different sovereign-duration risk. Finance Minister Roland Lescure is seeking to bring the deficit close to 5% of GDP, only a modest improvement from 5.1% in 2025. Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu warned that reliance on rolled-over legislation without a new finance bill could push the deficit to 6.5%. The 2027 budget must navigate a fragmented parliament and a presidential election due in April, leaving consolidation vulnerable to delay or disorder. Officials said issuance and auctions were functioning well, so the immediate issue is not market access; it is the future premium required if political compromise fails to produce a credible debt path.

Federal Reserve uncertainty shifted from the next decision toward governance and communication. June payroll growth of 57,000 and downward revisions had already softened the case for an immediate increase, even though lower participation complicated the fall in unemployment. During the holiday window, reporting that administration allies were exploring further ways to remove governors after the Supreme Court blocked the effort against Lisa Cook reopened the independence question. Chair Kevin Warsh’s reduced reliance on forward guidance adds another layer: fewer policy signals may make incoming data more influential, while political pressure could raise concern about how firmly inflation risks will be addressed.

Japan combined modest excess demand with visible pressure on its policy mix. The BOJ estimated a first-quarter output gap of 0.53%, down slightly from 0.57%, with positive contributions from both capital and labor. Its potential-growth estimate increased to 0.69% from 0.66%, although the central bank cautioned that both measures are sensitive to methodology. Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama reiterated readiness to act on disorderly currency moves and linked the rise in Japanese yields to U.S. policy signals, weak U.S. labor data and a soft domestic auction. The 10-year JGB yield reached 2.81%, its highest since 1996, showing that lower oil has not displaced fiscal, currency and buyer-base concerns.

China’s liquidity stance pointed to control rather than broad easing through the bond market. The PBOC bought a net 10 billion yuan of government debt in June, down from 50 billion yuan in May and the smallest amount in nine months. Its new overnight open-market operation gives policymakers a shorter instrument for steering funding, reducing the need to inject long-term liquidity through sovereign purchases. The combination can keep cash conditions ample while resisting excessive yield compression. It also separates support for a slowing domestic economy from a commitment to absorb duration at any price.

Primary credit remained accessible, with the more important signal coming from the structure and timing of prospective supply. CVC plans €1.2 billion of high-yield bonds after the summer to replace bank bridge financing for its acquisition of Italian food-ingredients producer Irca, alongside a revolving facility. The transaction would add leveraged-buyout supply to a European market still dominated by refinancing and repricing, even though speculative-grade demand has remained resilient. ICICI Bank is separately considering at least $500 million under its global medium-term-note program in coming weeks, using an RBI foreign-exchange swap concession designed to attract offshore capital. Neither planned deal establishes clearing terms yet; both show borrowers preparing to use open funding windows.

Emerging-market inflation and funding conditions stayed highly country-specific. Turkey’s June CPI rose 0.99% on the month and 32.11% from a year earlier, but food inflation of 35.45% and housing-and-utilities inflation of 45.14% show why cheaper energy alone may not anchor the disinflation path. At the same time, investors have diversified carry funding beyond the dollar into the euro, Canadian dollar and yen as uncertainty around U.S. policy and the dollar increased. That reduces dependence on one funding currency, though a renewed Fed tightening impulse could still narrow rate differentials and tighten conditions for high-yielding sovereigns. Brazil added a fiscal-monetary question through new credit and debt-renegotiation support for informal workers, even as officials argued that the measures would not weaken monetary restraint.

## Next Open-Session Watch

The first U.S. macro sequence begins Monday, July 6, at 10:00 a.m. ET with ISM services and the New York Fed’s Global Supply Chain Pressure Index. May trade follows Tuesday, alongside the June Survey of Consumer Expectations, and the June 16–17 FOMC minutes arrive Wednesday at 2:00 p.m. Softer services activity, cooling prices and contained logistics pressure would extend the message from weak payroll growth into the two- to five-year policy channel. Resilient demand or renewed price pressure, especially if the minutes reveal broad support for a 2026 increase, would restore a more restrictive interpretation and weaken the case that energy relief can carry the reopening.

Treasury’s announced supply calendar separates front-end cash demand from benchmark-duration absorption. Monday brings $92 billion of 91-day bills and $79 billion of 182-day bills; Tuesday adds $90 billion of 42-day bills, $52 billion of 364-day bills and a $58 billion three-year note. A $39 billion reopening of the 4.375% 10-year note follows Wednesday, then a $22 billion reopening of the 5.000% 30-year bond Thursday. Bid-to-cover, bidder allocation and post-auction performance will show whether the holiday settlement cycle is confined to cash management or reaches the long-end term premium. Broad participation would temper the supply concern; heavy dealer absorption or persistent cheapening would strengthen it.

Japan’s next releases determine whether the positive output gap is translating into durable income and inflation pressure. May provisional labor data arrive July 7, followed by June BOJ operations, government transactions and the May consumption index; the BOJ’s regional economic report is due July 9. Firm cash earnings, improving real wages and broader regional price pressure would raise the chance that normalization needs to proceed despite lower oil, with JGB duration and the yen most sensitive. Weak household income or consumption, combined with evidence that balance-sheet mechanics explain much of the liquidity change, would challenge the hawkish reading and reduce Japan’s contribution to global term-premium pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: The reopening faces softer immediate inflation pressure but a firmer policy-risk premium.
section_summaries.next_check: Monday’s services data will test whether weak hiring extends into the front-end policy path.
section_summaries.summary: With U.S. markets shut, the information window brought no domestic price discovery. Fading energy pressure and softer hiring eased near-term tightening urgency, but Japan’s normalization risks, selective Chinese liquidity support and questions over Fed independence left longer-duration compensation vulnerable.

section_summaries.market_read: Global easing hopes remain constrained by sovereign, currency and credibility risks.
section_summaries.next_check: European wage pass-through and France’s budget path will determine whether patience can persist.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Rates, Credit, And Sovereign Developments
section_summaries.summary: Cooling euro-area inflation and weaker French output favored ECB patience, but fiscal fragility in France and persistent Japanese bond pressure limited the duration-friendly signal. China’s shorter-term liquidity control and accessible primary credit showed funding support without a blanket bid for sovereign duration.

section_summaries.market_read: Data and auction reception will decide whether reopening pressure stays front-end or spreads.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch bid-to-cover, bidder allocation and post-auction trading at the 10- and 30-year sales.
section_summaries.summary: The next U.S. session must absorb a dense macro and auction calendar after the holiday. Services activity, supply-chain pressure and FOMC minutes will test the near-term policy path, while long-end auction demand and Japanese income data will reveal whether term-premium pressure broadens.

methodology_note: SIFMA recommends a full U.S. fixed-income market close for U.S. Independence Day (observed). No prior-session market levels are presented as current-day moves.

overall_summary.headline: Energy relief met persistent policy divergence ahead of the U.S. reopening
overall_summary.session_narrative: U.S. fixed-income markets were closed for U.S. Independence Day (observed), leaving no same-day U.S. price discovery. Across the Asia-to-Asia information window, fading energy pressure and soft U.S. hiring reduced near-term tightening urgency, while Japan’s positive output gap, weak yen and bond pressure preserved normalization risk. China favored precise funding support over broad duration demand, and renewed Fed-governance questions raised the premium on policy credibility. The next open session must reconcile easier near-term inflation pressure with dispersed, still-restrictive policy paths.
overall_summary.summary: Lower oil, softer U.S. hiring and cooling euro-area inflation eased near-term tightening pressure. Japan’s normalization risk, China’s controlled liquidity support and uncertainty around Fed governance kept global duration compensation exposed.

watch_events.market_implication: A hawkish mix would reinforce restriction in two- to five-year yields; softness would ease it.
watch_events.market_trigger: Resilient demand, renewed price pressure or broad support for a 2026 rate increase.
watch_events.title: U.S. services, inflation and FOMC minutes
watch_events.watch_line: Track services demand, price pressure, logistics conditions and the FOMC’s June debate.

watch_events.market_implication: Weak demand would lift long-end term-premium pressure; broad participation would temper it.
watch_events.market_trigger: Heavy dealer absorption or persistent cheapening at the 10- and 30-year reopenings.
watch_events.title: Treasury benchmark supply absorption
watch_events.watch_line: Assess bid-to-cover, bidder allocation and post-auction performance across the sales.

watch_events.market_implication: That mix would raise BOJ normalization risk, pressuring JGB duration and keeping the yen highly sensitive.
watch_events.market_trigger: Firm cash earnings, improving real wages and broader regional price pressure across Japan.
watch_events.title: Japan income and normalization test
watch_events.watch_line: Follow cash earnings, real wages, consumption and the BOJ’s regional price assessment.
